title: 10 best herbal teas for eye twitching
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye twitching are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the involuntary muscle spasms around the eyes. These teas often include natural ingredients known for their calming and soothing properties, such as chamomile, lavender, and valerian root. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, these herbal teas can help alleviate eye twitching and improve overall eye health. For instance, chamomile tea is widely recognized for its ability to soothe the nervous system, making it an ideal choice for those experiencing eye twitching due to anxiety or tension. Similarly, lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye irritation and inflammation, further contributing to the relief of eye twitching. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their quality of life, as they no longer have to deal with the discomfort and embarrassment caused by persistent eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tea helps with eye twitching because it contains high levels of antioxidants, including beta-carotene and vitamin C, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. These antioxidants can help improve circulation and reduce tension in the eye muscles, which can help alleviate eye twitching. Additionally, borage tea contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe irritated nerves and muscles. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, borage tea can help alleviate eye twitching and provide relief for those who experience this uncomfortable and sometimes painful condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Borage tea contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) which helps reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating eye twitching caused by irritation or inflammation.
Nervine: Borage tea has calming effects on the nervous system due to its content of essential fatty acids, alleviating eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
Antioxidant: Borage tea is rich in antioxidants like betaine and vitamin E, which help protect the eyes from oxidative damage, reducing the risk of eye twitching.
Diuretic: Borage tea promotes diuresis and helps eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing eye twitching caused by fluid retention or edema.

Nutrient-rich: Borage tea contains essential vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, and iron, which support overall eye health and help prevent eye twitching caused by nutrient deficiencies.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, borage flowers help reduce inflammation and may alleviate eye twitching.
Leaves: Containing essential fatty acids, borage leaves can help soothe eye muscles and potentially reduce eye twitching.
Seeds: High in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), borage seeds can help decrease inflammation and irritation associated with eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh borage leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and dirt.
Rinse the harvested leaves under cold running water, then let them air dry for 5 minutes.
Crush or chop the dried borage leaves to release their essential oils, then place them in a teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the crushed leaves, cover the teapot, and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent leaves, and consume it while still warm, up to three times a day to alleviate eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with eye twitching because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms, including those in the eye. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation. Additionally, pot marigold tea contains calming properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to eye twitching. Drinking pot marigold tea regularly may help relax the eye muscles and provide relief from persistent eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce eye twitching by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antispasmodic: Its antispasmodic properties, due to the presence of alkaloids, help relax the muscles around the eyes and alleviate twitching.
Calming: Pot marigold tea contains soothing compounds like saponins, which help reduce stress and anxiety, contributing to the relief of eye twitching.
Antioxidant: Rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, pot marigold tea aids in protecting the eyes from free radical damage, which may help prevent eye twitching.

Circulation improvement: The tea promotes blood circulation, thanks to active constituents like essential oils, ensuring proper nutrient supply to the eyes and reducing the likelihood of eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers of the pot marigold tea plant help reduce eye strain and inflammation, alleviating eye twitching.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the leaves of the pot marigold tea plant are used to soothe irritated eyes and prevent muscle spasms causing eye twitching.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, the seeds of the pot marigold tea plant are beneficial for treating eye twitching by promoting blood circulation and reducing tension in the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried pot marigold flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the pot marigold flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Cover the teapot or cup to retain the beneficial compounds and heat.
After steeping, remove the tea infuser or strain the liquid to discard the spent flowers.
Drink the pot marigold tea while warm, up to three times a day, for soothing relief of eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with eye twitching because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The herb, scientifically known as Euphrasia officinalis, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various eye ailments. The tea is thought to strengthen the eye muscles and improve overall eye health, which can help alleviate eye twitching. Additionally, eyebright tea is rich in tannins, which can have an astringent effect, potentially reducing spasms in the eyelid muscles and providing relief from eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce eye inflammation, which can alleviate eye twitching, thanks to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Astringent: The astringent properties of eyebright tea tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing eye twitching, due to tannins found in the plant.
Antispasmodic: Eyebright tea's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles around the eye, minimizing eye twitching, as it contains alkaloids like euphrine and tannins.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of eyebright tea provide pain relief for any discomfort caused by eye twitching, as it contains phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tea's antioxidant properties protect the eye from oxidative stress, promoting overall eye health and reducing eye twitching, due to its richness in phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, eyebright flowers help reduce eye inflammation and may alleviate eye twitching.
Leaves: High in tannins, eyebright leaves can soothe irritated eyes and potentially decrease muscle spasms causing eye twitching.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and nutrients, eyebright seeds can help improve eye health and reduce symptoms of eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried eyebright herb to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the herbal eyebright tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with eye twitching because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and relax the muscles around the eyes. The tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which have been used traditionally for their calming and relaxing effects. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, English lavender tea may help alleviate eye twitching and provide relief for tired or strained eyes. Additionally, lavender has been shown to help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to eye twitching. Drinking lavender tea or using lavender essential oil may help reduce the frequency and intensity of eye twitching, providing a natural and gentle solution for this common issue.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: English lavender tea helps relieve eye twitching by reducing muscle spasms, thanks to its active constituents, mainly linalool and linalyl acetate.
Nervine: Its calming effect on the nervous system helps alleviate eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety, due to the presence of volatile oils and tannins.
Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritation and inflammation in the eye area, containing compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in English lavender tea combats free radicals, which can contribute to eye twitching, with active constituents such as apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin.
Analgesic: Lavender tea's pain-relieving properties can help alleviate discomfort associated with eye twitching, attributable to its active constituents, including eucalyptol and camphor.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, lavender flowers help reduce inflammation and may alleviate eye twitching.
Leaves: Containing calming properties, lavender leaves can help reduce stress and anxiety, which may help minimize eye twitching.
Buds: High in essential oils, lavender buds can help soothe the eyes and potentially reduce eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature, ensuring it's not too hot for the eyes.
Use a clean eye dropper to apply the cooled lavender tea to the affected eye, blinking gently to distribute the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with eye twitching because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. These properties may help alleviate tension and stress in the eye muscles, which can be a common cause of eye twitching. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, potentially reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitching. Furthermore, lemon balm tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those who may be sensitive to caffeine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye twitching by alleviating any inflammation in the eye muscles, thanks to the presence of rosmarinic acid.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm tea, due to its rich flavonoid content, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress that can contribute to eye twitching.
Nervine: Lemon balm tea has a calming and soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety, attributed to its volatile oils and tannins.
Muscle relaxant: Lemon balm tea contains compounds that help relax muscles, including the eye muscles, which can reduce eye twitching, thanks to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
Sleep aid: Lemon balm tea promotes better sleep quality, and sufficient sleep can help prevent eye twitching, due to its calming and sedative properties from rosmarinic acid and citral.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for eye twitching due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for eye twitching as they contain flavonoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for eye twitching as they contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce muscle spasms.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for eye twitching as they contain essential oils with sedative properties that can help alleviate stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves to a teapot or infuser and pour the hot water over the leaves.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent lemon balm leaves.
Drink the warm tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help alleviate eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tea helps with eye twitching because it contains antispasmodic properties that can help to relax the muscles around the eye, reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitching. The tea is made from the leaves and roots of the passionflower plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including muscle spasms and nervous disorders. Maypop tea is thought to work by increasing the levels of a neurotransmitter called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which has a calming effect on the nervous system and can help to alleviate muscle spasms. Additionally, maypop tea contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and protect the eyes from damage.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Maypop tea helps relieve eye twitching by reducing muscle spasms, thanks to the active constituents like flavonoids and alkaloids.
Anti-anxiety: The calming effect of maypop tea on the nervous system, attributed to its chrysin content, can help alleviate stress-induced eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of maypop tea, due to its rich supply of antioxidants, help reduce inflammation that may contribute to eye twitching.
Sedative: Maypop tea promotes relaxation and sleep, reducing eye twitching caused by fatigue or lack of sleep, due to its sleep-inducing compounds like alkaloids.
Nutrient-rich: Maypop tea's high vitamin and mineral content, including B-complex vitamins, supports overall eye health and may help prevent eye twitching related to nutrient deficiencies.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help reduce eye twitching caused by stress and strain.
Seeds: Rich in vitamins and minerals that can help soothe the nerves and alleviate eye twitching.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye irritation and twitching.
Roots: Contain compounds that can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing eye twitching.
Bark: Has astringent properties that can help reduce eye puffiness and twitching.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the solid herb particles.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with eye twitching because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. These properties can help relax the muscles around the eye, including the ones responsible for eye twitching. Additionally, large-leaved lime tea is rich in vitamin C, which is essential for eye health and can help improve vision. Drinking this tea regularly may help reduce the frequency and intensity of eye twitching, providing relief for those who experience this uncomfortable and sometimes painful condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce eye twitching caused by inflammation.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including vitamin C, helps protect the eyes from damage and reduces the risk of eye twitching due to oxidative stress.
Nervine: Large-leaved lime tea contains nervine compounds that help soothe the nervous system, reducing eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
Muscle relaxant: The tea has muscle relaxant properties due to its magnesium content, which can help alleviate eye twitching caused by muscle spasms.
Nutrient-rich: Large-leaved lime tea is rich in essential vitamins and minerals, such as B vitamins and potassium, which contribute to overall eye health and can help prevent eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins, the leaves of the large-leaved lime tea plant can help alleviate eye twitching by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, the flowers of the large-leaved lime tea plant can help soothe eye twitching by reducing oxidative stress and calming the nervous system.
Seeds: Containing high levels of vitamins and minerals, the seeds of the large-leaved lime tea plant can help prevent eye twitching by nourishing the body and promoting overall health.
Bark: Abundant in essential oils, the bark of the large-leaved lime tea plant can help relieve eye twitching by promoting blood circulation and reducing muscle spasms.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried large-leaved lime flowers, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of chamomile flowers and 1 teaspoon of lemon balm leaves to the mixture, steeping for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea for added soothing properties, stirring until fully dissolved.
Drink 1 cup of the herbal tea, twice a day, until the eye twitching subsides.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tea helps with eye twitching because it contains valuable compounds like antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help reduce inflammation and tension in the eye muscles. Valerian root has natural sedative properties that can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation, thus minimizing eye twitching caused by anxiety or fatigue. Moreover, valerian tea may aid in stabilizing the neurotransmitter GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) in the brain, which can help regulate muscle contractions, including those in the eyelid. By consuming valerian tea, you can potentially soothe eye twitching and enjoy a more relaxed and comfortable visual experience.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Valerian tea contains alkaloids and iridoids that act as natural muscle relaxants, helping to alleviate eye twitching caused by muscle spasms.
Sedative: The tea has sedative properties due to its high content of valepotriates and volatile oils, which can help reduce eye twitching by calming the nervous system.
Antispasmodic: Valerian tea contains antispasmodic agents, such as valtrate and isovaltrate, that help alleviate eye twitching by reducing nerve impulses that cause muscle spasms.
Anxiolytic: Valerian tea has anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce anxiety and stress, contributing to the alleviation of eye twitching caused by nervous tension.
Analgesic: Valerian tea contains analgesic compounds, including valerenic acid and hesperidin, that can help manage eye twitching by reducing pain and discomfort associated with muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian tea plant contain high levels of isovaleric acid and valepotriates, which have been shown to help reduce eye twitching and provide a calming effect on the nervous system.
Flowers: The flowers of the valerian tea plant contain flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, making them useful in treating eye twitching.
Leaves: The leaves of the valerian tea plant contain volatile oils and alkaloids, which have a soothing effect on the nervous system and can help reduce eye twitching.
Seeds: The seeds of the valerian tea plant contain valepotriates and essential oils, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help alleviate eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried valerian root, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the valerian root, then let it cool down for a few minutes.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while still warm, 2-3 times a day to help alleviate eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena tea helps with eye twitching because it contains antispasmodic properties that can help to relax the muscles around the eye and reduce twitching. The tea is also known for its calming and soothing effects, which can help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of eye twitching. Additionally, verbena tea is rich in antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and promote overall eye health. Drinking verbena tea regularly can help to prevent eye twitching and maintain healthy eye function.
remedy_properties: Anti-spasmodic: Verbena tea has anti-spasmodic properties that help relax the muscles around the eye, reducing eye twitching. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as citral, limonene, and geraniol.

Nervine: Verbena tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this effect include verbascoside and ursolic acid.

Anti-inflammatory: Verbena tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling around the eye, alleviating eye twitching. The flavonoids, including luteolin and rutin, found in verbena are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antioxidant: Verbena tea contains antioxidants that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall eye health. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of phenolic acids, such as caffeic and chlorogenic acids.

Diuretic: Verbena tea has diuretic properties that help eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing the risk of fluid buildup around the eyes and, consequently, eye twitching. The active constituents responsible for this effect include verbascoside and aucubin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which may help reduce eye twitching by reducing inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress, a potential cause of eye twitching.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with calming properties that may help alleviate eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried lemon verbena leaves to a tea infuser or teapot and pour the hot water over the leaves.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes to allow the herb to release its beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup to remove the leaves.
Drink the warm verbena tea up to three times a day to help alleviate eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jujube tea helps with eye twitching because it contains valuable nutrients and antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and stress in the body. The tea is rich in vitamin B1, B2, and B3, which are essential for maintaining healthy nerve and muscle function. Additionally, jujube tea has calming properties that can help alleviate anxiety and promote better sleep, which can often contribute to eye twitching. The tea's antioxidant content, including flavonoids and saponins, can also help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage. By promoting overall eye health and reducing stress and inflammation, jujube tea can be a natural and effective remedy for eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Jujube tea can help relax the muscles around the eye, reducing twitching caused by muscle spasms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, which have muscle-relaxing effects.

Nervine properties: Jujube tea can help alleviate eye twitching by calming the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety. This is attributed to the active constituents, including jujuboside A and B, which have sedative and nervine effects.

Vitamin-rich: Jujube tea is rich in vitamins, such as vitamin B1, B2, and B3, which are essential for maintaining healthy eye function. These vitamins help support nerve function, reducing the likelihood of eye twitching.

Antioxidant properties: Jujube tea contains potent antioxidants that can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage. The antioxidants, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, can help maintain overall eye health and reduce the occurrence of twitching.

Mineral-rich: Jujube tea contains essential minerals, such as potassium, calcium, and magnesium, that contribute to proper muscle function. These minerals help regulate muscle contractions, reducing the likelihood of eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Contain saponins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and may alleviate eye twitching.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and vitamins, helping to soothe the eyes and potentially reduce nerve excitability causing twitching.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties, which may help alleviate eye twitching by reducing muscle spasms.
Bark: Contains tannins and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief for eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried jujube dates, bringing them to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10 minutes, then add 1 tablespoon of chamomile flowers.
Continue simmering for an additional 5 minutes, then remove from heat.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Strain the tea, discarding solids, and drink 2-3 times a day for relief of eye twitching.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye twitching with herbal teas is by using chamomile or valerian root tea. Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea, then gently apply it as a compress to the affected eye. These teas have natural properties that can help relax the muscles around the eye and reduce twitching. For best results, use organically grown herbs and drink the tea yourself to aid in relaxation. However, if twitching persists or becomes severe, consult a healthcare professional as it could indicate a more serious condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye twitching that are treated with herbal teas include nervousness, anxiety, and stress. Herbal teas such as chamomile, valerian root, and passionflower are known for their calming properties and can help alleviate symptoms. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory and relaxant effects, while valerian root tea is known for its sedative properties, which can promote relaxation and reduce eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety. Passionflower tea is also used to treat anxiety and restlessness, and can help alleviate eye twitching caused by these conditions. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedies.
